passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 16-18) In each of the following questions, a statement/group of statements is given followed by four conclusions. Choose the conclusion which logically follows from the given statements.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements A. In comparison with 2 decades ago, India is producing more two wheelers. B. The quality of such vehicles has also been improved. Conclusions I. We are exporting two wheelers. II. Our two wheeler industries have done commendable progress.

choices.a: Only Conclusion II can be drawn
choices.b: Only Conclusion I can be drawn
choices.c: Neither Conclusion I nor II can be drawn
choices.d: Both the Conclusions I and II can be drawn

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement Most dresses in that shop are expensive.

choices.a: Some dresses in that shop are expensive
choices.b: There are cheap dresses also in that shop
choices.c: Handloom dresses in that shop are cheap
choices.d: There are no cheap dresses available in that shop

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement A. All the students in my class are intelligent. B. Rashmi is not intelligent.

choices.b: Rashmi is not the student of my class

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements A. The constitution assures the fundamental rights. B. Parliament has right to amend the constitution. Conclusions I. Parliament included fundamental rights in the constitution. II. Parliament did not assure the fundamental rights.

choices.c: Both Conclusion I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A research study recorded that the number of unemployed educated youth was equal to the number of unemployed uneducated youth. It was concluded by the researchers that being educated does not enhance the probability of being employed. Which of the following information would be required to validate the above conclusion?

choices.a: The number of unemployed educated and uneducated people in other age groups
choices.b: The number of organisations employing youth
choices.c: The percentage of unemployment in educated youth versus percentage of unemployment in uneducated youth
choices.d: The percentage increase in number of educated youth versus last year

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: During the last summer vacation, Ankit went to a summer camp where he took part in hiking, swimming and boating. This summer, he is looking forward to a music camp where he hopes to sing, dance and learn to play the guitar. Based on the above information, four conclusions, as given below, have been made. Which one of these logically follows from the information given above?

choices.a: Ankit's parents want him to play the guitar
choices.b: Ankit prefers music to outdoor activities
choices.c: Ankit goes to some type of camp every summer
choices.d: Ankit likes to sing and dance

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Ten new TV shows started in January, 5 sitcoms, 3 drama and 2 news magazines. By April only seven of the new shows were still on, five of them being sitcoms. Based on the above information, four conclusions, as given below, have been made. Which one of these logically follows from the information given above?

choices.a: Only one news magazine show is still on
choices.b: Only one of the drama shows is still on
choices.c: At least one discontinued show was a drama

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Examine the following statements I. None but students are members of the club. II. Some members of the club are married persons. III. All married persons are invited for dance. Which one of the following conclusions can be drawn from the above statements?

choices.a: All students are invited for dance
choices.b: All married students of the club, are invited for dance
choices.c: All members of the club are married persons
